title: fix(deps): update module github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient to v1.10.0

comments.body: This update to go-dockerclient requires Go 1.20, while Fedora 37 only includes Go 1.19.
Ok we can bump the version as soon as @cevich migrates us to F38,f39

Oof, how the heck will I remember to do this.  Also, unless we keep this PR open, renovate will keep pestering about this update :confused: Hmmmm.  Is there a `go.mod` way to use different versions for different toolchains?

The only other idea I have is to add an exception to the renovate config w/ a FIXME comment, then pepper the image build scripts with links pointing at it.  That will shut up renovate (including for vulnerability alerts I think) and stands a chance of me remembering to re-enable it later.

Any better ideas?
Ed's [working on new images](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/302).  Adding a mention here in case it prompts anyone to take care of this PR after.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
There's new F38/39 images in podman in anyone wants to try bringing them here to address this issue.
Force-push: Updated images w/ new golang.

Assuming CI passes, this PR is ready to merge IMHO.

(I had to restart a few tasks due to github + GCE outages today)

/approve
/lgtm
force-push: rebased.
force-push: rebased
I think these are waiting for an upgrade to the version of golang.

> I think these are waiting for an upgrade to the version of golang.

Yes, my understanding is that version /is/ in the F39b images :thinking:
I think I got it now, the `go.sum` was wrong.  Added updates for golang 1.20 minimum in `Makefile` and `go.mod`.

Ugg, okay I'm not sure what [to do with this new error](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5235798375661568):

`go: go.mod file indicates go 1.20, but maximum version supported by tidy is 1.18`
Ahh, figured it out.  There's ANOTHER reference to `1.18` in `.cirrus.yml`.  That's super annoying, maybe that should just be `latest`?
Yes it should be latest.

> Yes it should be latest.

Done & force-pushed.
[Twice `[+3049s] panic: test timed out after 45m0s`](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6025754731872256), not sure what to do.  Increase timeout?
force-push: Increased unit test timeout to 55m
Nope, even at 55m, Unit tests [are still timing out](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6343222641819648?logs=unit_test#L495).  Not sure what to do here.

/approve
/lgtm
/hold

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *renovate[bot]*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
/lgtm
OMG, this is crazy, why does it constantly propose newer and newer updates.  This will never get in at this rate.  Maybe there's a way to stop the update-madness...
Force-push: Rebased, ran `make vendor-in-container`.

@rhatdan @nalind PTAL.  CI is green (finally).  Can we please merge this quickly?

I'm afraid Renovate (annoyingly) will decide it needs force-push again :confounded:
/lgtm
/hold cancel
@cevich if  you could figure out how to turn this off, so that it matches Podman, that would be great.

> if you could figure out how to turn this off, so that it matches Podman, that would be great.

I'm assuming "this" means "Renovate's annoying daily force-pushes".  It's very perplexing and frustrating for me because podman and buildah already share a common configuration :confused:  

Looking at the shared/common configuration, it says: Propose golang package version-bumps as soon as Renovate notices.  For golang digest-based package updates, restrict update PRs to once per month.

The only "special" thing in podman's setup (and not buildah's) is regarding `github.com/containers/*` packages.  For those, update proposals are restricted to once per day and updates to:

```
          "^github.com/containers/common",
          "^github.com/containers/image",
          "^github.com/containers/storage",
```

are grouped together into a single PR.

---

Re: force-push behavior, I'm similarly perplexed.  Supposedly adding a non-renovate-bot commit is suppose to stop that.  So is adding the `stop-updating` label.  Neither appears to be working :disappointed:  At this point I think this is a Renovate bug.  However a non-trivial reproducer will be needed to report that, and package version changes are out of our control.

---

The only easy option I see at the moment is to restrict golang packge version bumps based on a fixed schedule.  Like once per day or week or month or similar.  Could that suffice?

`@renovate renovate bot force-pushed the renovate/major-ci-vm-image branch 18 times, most recently from e0abfff to bfb8c04 
yesterday` in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5173 apparently it's all golang "tidy" stuff.

Perhaps that's what causes so many "updates".
I think once per/day would be fine.
